entries.definition: a soft mixture of lime or gypsum with sand and water that hardens as it dries, used for coating walls and ceilings
entries.exampleSentence: The old cottage had thick stone walls covered in lime plaster.

entries.definition: a small adhesive bandage for covering minor cuts and wounds (British English; US: band-aid)
entries.exampleSentence: She put a plaster on her finger after cutting it while chopping vegetables.

entries.definition: plaster of Paris — a white powder that sets hard when mixed with water, used for making casts and moulds
entries.exampleSentence: His broken arm was put in a plaster cast for six weeks.

entries.definition: to cover a surface with plaster; to cover something thickly or liberally with a substance; to stick things all over a surface
entries.exampleSentence: The walls of his bedroom were plastered with posters of rock bands.
